description_long: This is a World War II-era military bomber airplane with a camouflage paint scheme, featuring a long fuselage and four large propeller engines mounted on a straight, mid-mounted wing. The wings are broad and display roundel insignias, indicating Royal Air Force (RAF) markings. The tail section has a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and twin horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and is enclosed, with multiple windows for visibility. The landing gear is not visible in the images, suggesting it may be retractable. The overall structure is robust, with visible gun turrets and detailed engine nacelles.
description: A WWII-era RAF bomber airplane with four propeller engines, camouflage paint, and a conventional tail. Features include a mid-mounted straight wing, enclosed forward cockpit, and detailed engine nacelles.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, camouflage pattern, and RAF roundels on the wings.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the four propeller engines, cockpit placement, and fuselage markings.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view revealing the underwing structure, tail configuration, and engine placement.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view displaying the other side of the fuselage, wing details, and tail section.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines, nacelle-mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and twin horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable based on typical design.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
